39.9: And the inhabitants of the cities of Israel shall come out, and they shall set fire to the armor and to the shields and to the spears and to the bows and to the arrows and to the clubs of the hands and to the lances and they shall burn some of them in fire seven years
39.10: And they shall have no need for wood of the field or of the forest, but they shall burn some of their armor in fire, and they shall capture those who captured them, and they shall plunder those who plundered them, says THE LORD OF LORDS
39.11: In that day I shall give there a place to Gog for a tomb in Israel, the great valley that is to the East of the Sea, and they shall close off the valley and they shall bury Gog there and all his army, and it shall be called ‘The Valley of the End of Gog’
39.12: And for seven months, they of the house of Israel shall bury them, and the land shall be cleansed
39.13: And they shall bury all the people of the land, and it will be fame for them, in the day that I am glorified, says LORD JEHOVAH
39.14: And after seven months there shall be men constantly walking in the land, and burying those who were left in the land, so that it would be cleansed
39.15: And everyone who passes in the land and sees the bone of a son of man, he shall set up on its end a heap of stones, until those who bury come picking it up in the Valley of Gog
39.16: And the name of the city shall be named, “The Mighty”, and they shall cleanse the land
39.17: But you, son of man, say to all the birds of the sky and to every animal of the wilderness, ‘Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: be gathered and come from every place to the great sacrifice that I sacrifice to you in the mountains of Israel, and you shall eat flesh and drink blood
39.18: The flesh of mighty men you shall eat and the blood of Princes of the Earth you shall drink, of rams and of calves, and of hegoats and of bulls and the young bullocks of Bayshan, all of them
39.19: And you shall eat flesh and you shall be satisfied, and you shall drink blood, and you shall be drunk from the sacrifice that I sacrifice to you
39.20: And you shall be satisfied from my table: the flesh of horses and of their riders, and of mighty men and of all the warrior men’, says THE LORD OF LORDS
39.21: And I shall accomplish my glory among the nations, and all of the nations shall see the judgment that I have executed, and my hand that I have set upon them
39.22: And they of the house of Israel shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH their God, from that day and beyond
39.23: And the nations shall know that because of their evil the children of Israel were taken captive, for they have done evil to me, and I turned my face from them and I handed them over into the hands of those hating them, and all of them fell by the sword
39.24: And according to their abominations and according to their evil I have paid them, and I turned my face from them”
39.25: Because of this, thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: therefore I return the captivity of Yaqob and I show compassion on all the house of Israel, and I am zealous because of The Name of my holiness
39.26: And they shall bear all that they have done to me in their shame and their evil when they dwelt in their land in quietness, and there was none molesting them
39.27: Whenever I have gathered them from the nations and I have brought them from the cities of their enemies, and I am hallowed by them before the eyes of the many nations
39.28: And they shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH their God. I have caused them to be captured among the nations, and I shall gather them to their land, and I shall not leave some of them there
39.29: And I shall not turn my face from them, but I shall pour out my Spirit on all the house of Israel, says THE LORD OF LORDS”

40.1: In the twenty fifth year of our captivity, at the first of the year, in the tenth of the month in the fourteenth year after the city was defeated, on this day the hand of LORD JEHOVAH was upon me
40.2: And he brought me there in a vision of God, and he brought me to the land of Israel, and he set me on a mountain that was very high, and there was upon it, on its south side, like the edifice of a city
40.3: And he brought me there, and I saw a man, and his appearance was like the appearance of brass, and a cord of linen was in his hand, and a measuring reed, and he was standing in the gate
40.4: And that man said to me: ‘Son of man, see with your eyes and hear with your ears and put in your heart everything that I show you, because it is to show you I have come here, and everything that you see, show to the children of Israel’
40.5: And I saw a wall that encircled that house and a measuring reed in the hand of that man, and it was six cubits and a handbreadth, and he measured the width of the building, one reed, and its height, one reed
40.6: And he came to the gate that looks to the East and he went up on its steps and he measured the threshold of the gate, one reed to its width, and the other threshold, one reed for its width
40.7: And the porch, one reed for its length and one reed for its width, and five cubits between the door posts, and the threshold of the door between the door posts of the gate, one reed
40.8: (This redundant verse about the porch’s measurements according to the Hebrew is “He measured also the porch of the gate within, one reed.” see v.7, and is not found in the Peshitta Bible.)
40.9: And he measured the door posts of the gate, eight cubits, and the threshold of the gate from within, two cubits
40.10: And the inside of the eastern door, three from here and three from there, and one measure to their three, and one measure for the threshold that was from here and from there
40.11: And he measured the width of the entrance of the gate, ten cubits, and the length of the gate, thirteen cubits
40.12: And the border that was before the door post, a cubit from here and on that side, and the inside, six cubits from here and six cubits from there
40.13: And he measured the gate from its roof to its roof, the width twenty and five cubits, and a gate next to a gate
40.14: And he made before the face of the gate, sixty cubits, and before the front of the other outside gate, sixty cubits
40.15: And before the inner gate, fifty cubits
40.16: And windows that are spread out from within, and narrow ones from outside on planks, and upon the door posts of the gate, and windows that were surrounding them from within
40.17: And he brought me to the outer courtyard, and I saw thirty steps in a spiral, stepping one above another
40.18: And the steps were on the side of the gate that was against the length of the stairs
40.19: And he measured its width from before the door of the courtyard, a hundred cubits to the East and to the North
40.20: And the gate that is before it from the north of the courtyard, he measured its length and its width
40.21: And its measurement, three from here and three from there, like the measurement of the first door, fifty cubits in its length, and its width, twenty cubits and five
40.22: And its windows and its lintels were like the measure of the gate that faces to the East, and in seven steps going up to it; and a vestibule is before them
40.23: And the gates of the inner courtyard that were opposite the gate of the northeast, he measured from gate to gate, a hundred cubits
40.24: And he carried me to the road of the south, and I saw the gate of the south, and he measured its gate posts and its pillars according to these measurements
40.25: And it has windows and its gate posts circling like these windows, and fifty cubits was its length and twenty and five its width
40.26: And with seven steps going up to it, and a vestibule was before it, and palm trees were with it, one from here and one from there, by its gate posts
40.27: And the gates of the inner court which is in the way of the south, he measured from gate to gate, a hundred cubits
40.28: And he brought me to the inner court in the gate of the south, and he measured the southern gate according to these dimensions
40.29: And the measurements of its inside and its porches and its vestibules are like these, and it has windows and porches in a circle, fifty cubits its length and twenty and five its width
40.30: And its porches circling it, their length was twenty and five cubits and their width five cubits
40.31: And the porches of the outer courtyard had palm trees by them, and their eight steps
40.32: And he brought me to the inner courtyard in the way of the East, and he measured the gate like these measurements
40.33: And the inside and its porches and its vestibules and windows are in it, and its porches in a circle; the length was fifty cubits and the width twenty and five
40.34: And the vestibules of the outer courtyard, and the palm trees were in its porches, from here and there, and it had eight steps
40.35: And he brought me to the north gate, and he measured according to these measurements
40.36: And the inside and its porches and its posts and the windows went around it; its length was fifty cubits and its width twenty and five
40.37: And the porches of the outer courtyard, and palm trees were in its porches, here and there, and it had eight steps
40.38: And there was a chamber door in the porches of the gate, and there they lay burning peace offerings
40.39: And in the porches of the gate were two tables here and two tables there, to kill upon them burning peace offerings, and offerings, and the purification of sin
40.40: And in the side of the north gate, outside of the burning peace offerings, were two tables, and in the other side of the porches, two tables
40.41: Four tables here and four tables there, in the porches of the gate of the land, and they are the eight tables upon which they slaughter
40.42: And four tables of cut stone for a burnt peace offering, its length a cubit and a half, and its width a cubit and a half, and lying upon them are the instruments which those slaughtering burning peace offerings and sacrifices have by them
40.43: And their edges were a handbreadth going around, and they were laying upon the table the flesh of offerings
40.44: And outside of the inner gate were arched chambers of Princes in the inner court, in the side of the north gate, and their faces to the south, one from the side of the eastern gate which was against the north wind
40.45: And he said to me: “This chamber that faces to the south is for the Priests who keep guard of the house
40.46: And the chamber that faces the north is for the Priests, the sons of Tsadoq, who keep guard of the altar, who come near from the sons of Levi, to do the service of LORD JEHOVAH
40.47: And he measured the courtyard, its length a hundred cubits, and its width a hundred cubits, foursided, and the altar that was before the house
40.48: And he brought me to the porch of the house, and he measured a post five cubits from here and five cubits from there, and the width of the gate was three cubits from here and three cubits from there
40.49: And the length of the porch, twenty cubits, and its width, eleven cubits, and by steps they were going up to it, and the pillars stood by the door posts, one here and one there

41.1: And he brought me to the Temple, and he measured a post: its width was six cubits from here, and its width, six cubits from there
41.2: And the width of the Tabernacle and the width of the door, ten cubits, and the sides of the door, five cubits from here and five cubits from there, and he measured its length forty cubits, and its width twenty cubits
41.3: And he entered within and he measured into the doorway two cubits, and the door six cubits, and its width seven cubits
41.4: And he measured its length twenty cubits, and its width twenty cubits next to the temple, and he said to me: “This is The Holy of Holies”
41.5: And he measured the wall of the house, six cubits, and the width of the side, seven cubits circling the house
41.6: And the side partitions, partition on partition, thirty three of them, and the side partitions entered into the wall of the house in a circle that were enclosed and separate from the wall
41.7: And the partitions were joined above that encircled the house above, that enlarged the house from above, and from beneath; they would be going up to the middle ones, and from the middle ones to the upper rooms
41.8: And I saw the height of the house as a circle, and they were separate one from another, a full reed of six cubits
41.9: And the width of the wall of the side partition outside was five cubits, and from within, five cubits
41.10: And the width between the chambers which encircled the house was twenty cubits
41.11: And the door of the side partition that was separate, one to the south, and one to the north, and the width of the place that was separate, five cubits
41.12: And the building that was before the partition space in the west gate: its width was seventy cubits and the width of the wall of the building was five cubits in a circle, and the length was ninety cubits
41.13: And he measured the house, its length a hundred cubits, and the length of the separate partition of the building of the wall was a hundred cubits
41.14: And the width next to the house and the eastern partition, a hundred cubits
41.15: And he measured the length of the building that was before the separate partition, and in its place and its porches from here and from there, a hundred cubits, and the inner Temple and the porches of the gates of the courtyard
41.16: And the door posts and the windows and the porches going around their three gates before the wood as it went around, and its length was as far as the windows
41.17: And the windows were covered above the door of the inner house, and outside to all the walls in a circle. He measured from outside and from within
41.18: And he made Cherubim and palm trees, and a palm tree between a Cherub and a Cherub, and two faces to each Cherub
41.19: And the face of a son of man toward palm trees, from here and from there, was made for all the house in a circle
41.20: From the ground and up to above the doors, the Cherubim and palm trees were made
41.21: And the wall of the Temple was square, and the front of The Holy Place of the vision was like the appearance of an altar of wood
41.22: Three cubits was its height, and its length, two cubits, and it had horns on it, and its length and its railings of wood. And he said to me: “This is the table that is in the presence of LORD JEHOVAH
41.23: And two doors each for a folding door
41.24: Two for one folding door and two for another folding door
41.25: Cherubim and palm trees were made on the doors of the temple like those made in the walls and the face of the porch, overlaid with wood from the outside
41.26: And narrow windows and palm trees were here and there on the sides of the porch, and the sides of the house were overlaid with boards

42.1: And he brought me to the outer courtyard to the way north, and he brought me to the chamber next to the separate partition and next to the north building
42.2: Next to the length of a hundred forearms of the northern gate, and the width of fifty cubits
42.3: Opposite the door of the inner courtyard and opposite the steps of the outer courtyard of the building, one upon another for each of three stories ”
42.4: And in front of the chambers of the walking place was ten cubits in width, and the length a hundred cubits, and the door of the walking place was toward the north
42.5: And the chambers above were small, because the stairs were entering into them
42.6: For they were trisected, and there were no pillars for them like the pillars of the courtyard, therefore they were smaller than the lower ones and the middle ones
42.7: And in the outside wall that was opposite the rooms, the length was fifty cubits
42.8: Because the length of the rooms of the outer courtyard was fifty cubits, and next to the Temple it was a hundred cubits
42.9: And the door of the room of the eastern entrance that enters to the outer courtyard
42.10: In the width of the wall of the courtyard, the eastern entrance before the separate partition and before the building of the rooms
42.11: And the walkway in front of them and the appearance of the rooms which were on the way of the north, their length was like their width, and all their exits and their entrances and their appearance and their doors
42.12: Were like the doors of the rooms which were in the way of the south door, at the head of the way, the way at the entrance of the wall which is in the way of the eastern valley as it comes
42.13: And he said to me: “The north chambers and the chambers of the south that are next to the partition, these are the chambers where the Priests eat, they who offer to LORD JEHOVAH the Holy of Holies. There they set the Holy of Holies and fine wheat flour and the purification of sin offerings, because that place is holy
42.14: And when the Priests enter, they shall not go out from the Holy Place to the outer courtyard, but there they lay their garments in which they serve, because they are Holiness, and they wear other garments and bring offerings in the place of the people
42.15: And he finished the measurements of the inner house and he brought me to the way of the gate that faces to the East and he measured it as a circle
42.16: And he measured to the east wind on a measuring reed, five hundred reeds
42.17: And he measured to the north wind on a measuring reed, five hundred reeds in a circle
42.18: And he measured to the south wind, five hundred reeds on a measuring reed
42.19: And he measured to the west wind, five hundred reeds on a measuring reed
42.20: To the four winds he measured it, and the wall in a circle: its length five hundred and its width five hundred, that will separate the Holy Place and the common

43.1: And he carried me to the gate that looks to the East
43.2: And, behold, the glory of the God of Israel came from the way of the East, and his voice like the sound of many waters, and the Earth shone brightly from his glory
43.3: Like the appearance that I saw when I came to destroy the city, and like the appearance that I saw at River Kebar, and I fell on my face
43.4: And the glory of LORD JEHOVAH was over the house in the way of the gate that faces to the East
43.5: And The Spirit picked me up and he brought me into the inner courtyard, and I saw that the house of LORD JEHOVAH was filled with his glory
43.6: And I heard the thing that he said to me from within the house, and a man was standing with me
43.7: And he said to me: “Son of man, the place of my throne and the place of the soles of my feet where I dwell among the children of Israel to eternity, and they of the house of Israel shall not be defiled again and they shall not pollute the name of my holiness again, they and their Kings in their whoredom, and by the corpses of their Kings and by their idols
43.8: For they were making their gates by the side of my gates, and their borders on the side of my borders, and a wall was between me and them, and they polluted the name of my holiness in the evil that they had done, and I consumed them in my anger
43.9: Therefore let them put their fornication and the corpses of their kings far away from before me, and I shall dwell among them for eternity
43.10: But you, son of man, show this house to the house of Israel, and they shall be restrained from their evil that they have done to me, and they shall measure its likeness
43.11: And if they are restrained from the thing that they had done, show them the image of the house and its preparation and its entrances and its exits and all its images and all its laws and all its forms, and draw for their eyes, so that they will keep all its forms and its entire design, and they shall make them
43.12: This is the appearance of the house on the top of the mountain, and all its borders as they are circling The Holy of Holies
43.13: These are the measurements of the altar by the forearm, which is a cubit and a handbreadth, its hollow of a forearm cubit, and the railing is a span to its edge in a circle, and so in its side
43.14: And from the ground and unto the lower railing, two cubits, and its width one forearm, and from the lower railing and unto the upper, four cubits, and the width, a cubit
43.15: And the altar was four cubits, and from the altar and above, four horns
43.16: And the length of the altar, twelve cubits, and its width, twelve, square on its four sides
43.17: And its railing, fourteen cubits its length, and fourteen its width on its four sides, and its railing and the border that was around it was half of a cubit, and its hollow is round, and its steps face to the East
43.18: And he said to me: “Son of man, thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: these are the laws of the altar in the day that it is made. They shall offer upon it burnt offerings and they shall pour blood upon it
43.19: And I shall give to the Priests and to the Levites who are of the seed of Tsadoq, who come near to me, says THE LORD OF LORDS, to serve to me a bull, son of a herd, for the purification of sin
43.20: And take from his blood and sprinkle on the four horns of the altar and on the four corners of the railing and on the border that is around it, and sprinkle upon it, and it shall be clean
43.21: And take the bull of purification, and they shall offer it up on the side of the house outside of the Sanctuary
43.22: And on the second day, bring for purification a kid of the goats without a flaw in it, and they shall purify the altar as they purified it with the bull
43.23: And when you have finished purging, bring a bull, a son of a herd without a flaw in it, and a ram from a flock without a flaw in it
43.24: And bring them before LORD JEHOVAH, and the Priests shall cast salt upon them, and they shall bring them as burning peace offerings to LORD JEHOVAH
43.25: You shall bring one kid per day, seven days, for purification, and one bull, son of a herd, and a ram from a flock without a flaw in it
43.26: They shall be offering seven days, and they shall cleanse the altar and they shall consecrate their hands
43.27: And when the days are finished from the seventh day and beyond, then the Priests shall bring to the altar their burning peace offerings and their offerings, and I shall be pleased with them, says THE LORD OF LORDS”

44.1: And he returned me to the way of the outer gate of the Holy Place that faces to the East, and I saw it that it was shut
44.2: And LORD JEHOVAH said to me: “This gate shall be shut and it shall not be opened, and no man shall enter by it, because LORD JEHOVAH, The God of Israel, enters by it. It shall be shut
44.3: The Governor shall sit in it and he shall eat bread before LORD JEHOVAH; he shall enter from the way of the gate of the porch, and he shall come forth from it
44.4: And he brought me to the way of the northern gate that was before the house, and I saw that the house was filled with the glory of LORD JEHOVAH, and I fell on my face
44.5: And LORD JEHOVAH said to me: “Son of man, put into your heart and see with your eyes and hear with your ears everything that I speak with you, and all the laws of the house and all its designs, and put into your heart the entrances of the house and the exits of the Holy Place
44.6: And say to the rebellious house of Israel: “Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: ‘Are all your abominations not enough for you, house of Israel?
44.7: But you also bring from the children of foreigners who are uncircumcised in their heart and uncircumcised in their flesh, and they are in my Holy Place, and they pollute my house, and you offer with my bread the fat and the blood, and you nullify my covenant with all your abominations
44.8: And you do not keep guard of my Holy Place, but you have made the Keepers of the Guard of my Holy Place your Guards
44.9: Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: no son of foreigners who is uncircumcised in his heart and uncircumcised in the flesh shall enter into my Holy Place, and no son of foreigners that is among the children of Israel
44.10: Neither the Levites who separated from me when the sons of Israel went astray from me after their idols, and they bore their evil
44.11: And they were in my Holy Place and they were ministering in the gates of the house. They shall slaughter burning peace offerings and sacrifices of the people, and they shall stand before them and shall minister to them
44.12: Because they ministered to them before their idols, and they were a stumbling block of evil to the children of Israel; because of this, I have lifted my hands against them, says THE LORD OF LORDS, and they bear their evil
44.13: And they shall not approach before me to minister to me, neither shall they come near to all my holy things among The Holy of Holies, and in their shame they shall bear all of it and all their abominations that they have done
44.14: And I shall make them the Keepers of the Guard of the house, and workers of the service of everything that is in it
44.15: And the Priests and the Levites, the sons of Tsadoq, who kept guard of my Holy Place when the sons of Israel went astray from me, they shall approach me to minister to me, and they shall stand before me and shall offer to me the fat and the blood, says THE LORD OF LORDS
44.16: Those shall enter into my Holy Place, and they shall approach my table so that they shall serve me, and they shall keep my observances
44.17: And when they enter into the gate of the court, they shall wear inner garments of linen, and they shall not wear any wool, also when they serve in the gate of the inner courtyard
44.18: Also when inside, they shall put a turban of linen on their heads, and they shall tie a girdle of linen on their waists
44.19: And when they go out to the outer courtyard to the people, they shall strip their garments in which they minister, and they shall leave them in the chambers of Holiness, and they will wear other garments, so that they will not hallow the people in their own garments
44.20: They shall not shave their heads; they shall not grow their curls, but they shall surely crop their heads
44.21: And the Priests shall not drink wine when they enter the inner courtyard
44.22: And they shall not take for themselves a widow or a divorced woman as wives, but they shall take only a virgin of the seed of Israel, or a widow who is the widow of a Priest
44.23: And they shall teach my people between holiness and common, and they shall make known between impure and pure
44.24: And they will arise to judge for judgments, and they shall judge in my judgments, and they shall keep my law and my commandments in all the times of my appointed meetings, and they shall hallow my Sabbaths
44.25: And they shall not come to the dead body of a son of man, that they shall not be defiled, but only for his father, or for his mother, or for his son, or for his daughter, or for his brother, or for his virgin sister who had no husband, may they be defiled
44.26: And after he is defiled, he will number seven days for himself, and then he shall be purified
44.27: And in the day that he enters the Holy Place to the inner courtyard to serve in Holiness, he shall offer for his purification, says THE LORD OF LORDS
44.28: And it shall be their inheritance. I am their inheritance, and you shall not give a portion to them in Israel. I am their portion
44.29: The meal offering and the purification of the sin offering they shall eat, and every devoted thing that is in Israel shall be theirs
44.30: And the first of all first born ones of all your offering that you offer shall be the Priests’, and the first of your dough give to the Priests that a blessing may be in your houses
44.31: And anything that suffers pain or is broken, of bird or of beast, the Priests shall not eat

45.1: And whenever you divide the land, separate a division to LORD JEHOVAH, and hallow some land, and its length will be twenty and five thousand, and its width ten thousand, and all its borders shall be holy around it
45.2: And there shall be for the Holy Place five hundred by five hundred, and there shall be an enclosure as a circle, and fifty cubits for its open courtyard
45.3: And from this the measure is: the length twenty and five thousand, and the width ten thousand, and The Holy of Holies shall be in it
45.4: And the holy place of land shall be for the Priests that serve in the holy place of LORD JEHOVAH and are brought near for his service to LORD JEHOVAH, and they will have a place for houses, and a holy place for the Holy Place
45.5: And the length shall be twenty and five thousand and the width ten thousand, and twenty houses will be an inheritance for the Levites who serve in The House
45.6: The inheritance of the city is five thousand in width and twenty and five thousand in length. It shall be next to the division of the Holy Place for all of the house of Israel
45.7: And for the Ruler of the people, for a division of Holiness and for an inheritance of the city, from the East and from the West, and the length shall be equal to the division from East to West
45.8: It shall be an inheritance to the children of Israel, and the Rulers of my people shall not harm them again, and they shall give the land to the children of Israel for their tribes
45.9: Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: “It is enough for you Princes of Israel! Put away from you plunder and spoil and execute judgment and righteousness and remove your oppression from my people, says THE LORD OF LORDS!
45.10: You shall have a scale of truth and a measure of truth and a weight of truth
45.11: Make ready a measure and a weight that you will be taking up tithes, one part of ten, equally
45.12: And there shall be twenty shekels, plus a shekel from them twenty and five minas, plus a shekel of fifteen shall be for you one mina
45.13: This is the offering: offer one of six parts of a cor of wheat and one of six parts of a cor of barley
45.14: And oil of ten measures that is a cor. They shall receive a tenth measure each
45.15: And one ewe of every two hundred sheep of Israel for the meal offering, and for the offering, and for the burning peace offering that will purify them, says THE LORD OF LORDS
45.16: All the people of the land shall separate this gift for the Ruler of Israel
45.17: And there shall be burning peace offerings and fine wheat flour and a drink offering for the Ruler on the first of the month, and on the Sabbaths, and in all the feasts of the house of Israel. He shall offer the purification of sin and fine wheat flour and a burning peace offering, a gift that purifies the children of Israel
45.18: Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: “In the first day of the first month, take a bull, a son of a herd that has no flaw in it and purify the Holy Place
45.19: And the Priest shall take from the blood of purification and he shall sprinkle on the door posts of the house and upon the four corners of the altar and upon the posts of the gate of the inner court
45.20: And thus a man who does wrong and goes astray shall be doing on the seventh of the month, and they shall cleanse the house
45.21: And on the fourteenth of the first month shall be Passover and the feast for you, and you shall eat unleavened bread seven days
45.22: And the Leader shall offer a bull in that day for the sake of his soul, and for the sake of all the people of the land, for the purifying of sin
45.23: And during the seven days of the feast he shall offer burning peace offerings to LORD JEHOVAH, seven oxen and seven rams without a flaw in them, for the purification of sin, and a male kid of the goats by day
45.24: And a bushel basket of fine wheat flour for the bull, and a bushel basket for the ram, and a hin of oil for a bushel basket
45.25: And in the fifteenth of the seventh month, he shall make a feast like these seven days according to the purification of sin, and according to burning peace offering, and according to the meal offering, and according to the oil

46.1: Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: “The gate of the inner courtyard that faces to the East shall be shut the six days in which work is done, and on the day of the Sabbath it shall be opened, and on the day of the first moon it shall be opened
46.2: And the Ruler will enter in the entrance of the porch of the gate from outside, and he shall stand by a post of the door, and the Priests shall bring the burning peace offerings and his gifts, and he shall worship at the post of the door, and he shall go out and the gate will not be shut until evening
46.3: And the people of the land shall worship in the entrance of that gate on the Sabbaths that are on the beginnings of the months, before LORD JEHOVAH
46.4: And burning peace offerings that the Ruler brings before LORD JEHOVAH on the day of the Sabbath: six lambs without a flaw in them, and a ram without a flaw in it
46.5: And he shall make a bushel basket of fine wheat flour for the bull, and a bushel basket for the ram, and for the lambs, according to what has arrived in his hands, but a hin of oil to a bushel basket
46.6: And on the first day of the month, a bull without a flaw in it, and six lambs without a flaw in them, and a ram without a flaw in it
46.7: And he shall make a bushel basket of fine wheat flour for the bull, and a bushel basket for the ram, and for the lambs, according to what has come into his hands, but a hin of oil for each bushel basket
46.8: And whenever the Ruler enters the porch of the gate, he shall enter and he shall go out by it
46.9: And when the people of the land enter before LORD JEHOVAH in the time of the feasts, he who enters into the north gate to worship shall go out the south gate, and he who enters the south gate shall go out into the north gate, and he shall not return by the gate that he enters, but he shall go out opposite it
46.10: But the Ruler who is among you, by which gate he enters he shall go out
46.11: And at the time of the feasts there shall be a meal offering: a bushel basket with the bull, and a bushel basket with the ram, and with the lambs, a man according to what has come into his hands, but a hin of oil for each bushel
46.12: And whenever the Leader brings burning peace offerings or gifts set apart for LORD JEHOVAH, the door that faces to the East that was shut shall be opened to him, and he shall bring the burning peace offerings and his gifts, and they shall shut the door, and then he shall go out as he did on the day of the Sabbath
46.13: And he shall be bringing a lamb, a son of a year without a flaw in it, a burning peace offering to LORD JEHOVAH by day, from dawn to dawn
46.14: And he shall offer fine wheat flour upon it from dawn to dawn, one sixth of a bushel basket, and one third hin of oil, to lay the meal offering for LORD JEHOVAH, the constant law to eternity
46.15: And they shall bring lambs and fine wheat flour and oil, from dawn to dawn, a burning peace offering constantly
46.16: Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: “When the Leader will give a gift to one of his sons, it will be his inheritance to his children
46.17: And when he will give a gift from his inheritance to one of his Servants, it will be his until the year that he is freed; it shall be an inheritance of the Leader, but that of his children shall be theirs
46.18: And the Leader shall not take from the inheritance of the people, and he shall not defraud them of their inheritance, but he shall cause his children to inherit from his inheritance, that my people will not be scattered, any man from his inheritance”
46.19: And he brought me into the entrance that was upon the side of the gate, of the inner room of Holiness of the Priests that faces the north, and I saw that there was a place at its border from the West
46.20: And he said to me: “This is the place in which the Priests boil the purification of sin, and in which they bake the meal offering, that they will not go out to the outer courtyard, and they will hallow the people”
46.21: And he brought me to the outer courtyard, and he brought me over the four sides of the courtyard, and I saw a courtyard at the side of the courtyard, and another court at the other side of the courtyard
46.22: On the four sides of the courtyard were small courtyards, forty cubits their length, and thirty their width
46.23: And chambers surrounded the four of them
46.24: And he said to me: “This is the house of cooks in which the ministers of the house boil the sacrifice of the people

47.1: And he moved and he brought me to the door of the house, and I saw the waters that went out from under the eastern porch of the house, and those waters descended from the side of the house, from the south of the altar
47.2: And he brought me out by the north gate, and he took me around from outside by the gate of the courtyard that faces to the East, and I saw the waters going out its right side
47.3: And while a glorious man went out and a measure in his hand, also he measured one thousand cubits and he brought me into the water unto the ankles
47.4: And he measured one thousand cubits and he brought me into water unto the knees, and he measured one thousand cubits and he brought me in water unto the groin
47.5: And he measured one thousand cubits, a stream that no man can cross, because the waters had increased, and the stream had become powerful, and no man was able to cross it
47.6: And he said to me: “Behold, son of man!” And he carried me and he set me on the bank of the torrent
47.7: And as I sat, I saw on the bank of the torrent very many trees, on this and on that side
47.8: And he said to me: “These waters that go to Galilee to the east and go down to the north, and they go down into the Sea into stagnant water, and they sweeten the waters
47.9: And every living soul that is brought forth where the waters of the torrent are, lives, and there are many fish, because of those waters that go down there, and they sweeten the waters
47.10: And fishermen were standing by it from EynGad and unto EynEglyn. It shall be the spreading the nets, and there shall be many fish in it, like that of the Great Sea
47.11: And its exits and its entrances shall not be sweetened, but they shall be salt
47.12: And in the torrent, all food trees shall grow up on its sides, from here and there. Their leaves shall not fall and their fruit shall not rot, and on the first of every month they shall be bringing new fruit, because the waters that they drink are going out from The Holy Place. Their fruit shall be for eating, and their leaves for healing
47.13: Thus says THE LORD OF LORDS: ‘The valleys of the border which they inherit for the land, for the twelve tribes of Israel, and for a portion of Yoseph
47.14: For you shall inherit it, a man from his brother, for I have lifted my hands to give it to your fathers, and the land shall be divided to you for an inheritance
47.15: And this is the border of the land to the north of the Great Sea: the road of Khethrun that enters to Tsadad
47.16: And Khamath and Beruth and Sepharvaim that is between the borders of Darmsuq and the borders of Khamath and Khatsar, the middle of which is on the border of Khuran
47.17: And the border will be from the Sea to Khatsar Ainan that is on the border of Darmsuq and Tsephon that is in the North, which is on the border of Khamath; this is the north side
47.18: And from the east wind from between Khuran and from between Darmsuq and from between Galeed and the land of Israel, and Jordan the border on the Sea east of Tamar; this is the East side
47.19: And the south wind of Tamar and unto the waters of Maribath Qadish, the inheritance of the Great Sea
47.20: And the border unto Nakakh that is in the entrance of Khamath; this is the West side
47.21: And you shall divide this land for the tribes of Israel
47.22: And when you shall divide it as an inheritance for yourselves, and those who are converted to me who dwell among you, and they give birth to children among you, they also shall be as the children of Israel and they shall divide the inheritance with you among the tribes of Israel
47.23: And the tribe that has in it one who is converted to me shall give him an inheritance in it, says THE LORD OF LORDS

48.1: And these are the names of the tribes from the end of the North on the side of the road of Khethrun that is in the entrance of Khamath and of Khetsar Ainan, and the border of Darmsuq that is in the North that is upon the side of Khamath; this is the East side of the Sea of Dan
48.2: And opposite the border of Dan from the east wind and unto the west wind of Ashayr
48.3: And opposite the border of Ashayr from the east wind and unto the west wind of Naphtali
48.4: And opposite the border of Naphtali from the east wind and unto the west wind of Menashe
48.5: And opposite the border of Menashe from the east wind and unto the west wind of Rubil
48.6: And opposite the border of Rubil from the east wind and unto the west wind of Ephraim
48.7: And opposite the border of Ephraim from the east wind and unto the west wind of Yehuda
48.8: And opposite the border of Yehuda from the east wind and unto the west wind there shall be a division that you dedicate; the width twenty and five thousand and the length like one of the portions, and from the east wind and unto the west wind shall be The Holy Place within it
48.9: The division that you dedicate to LORD JEHOVAH: the length, twenty and five thousand, and the width, ten thousand
48.10: And a division of Holiness shall be for the Priests to the North, twenty and five thousand, and the width, ten thousand, and to the West, ten thousand, and to the East, ten thousand, and the South, twenty and five thousand, and the holy place of LORD JEHOVAH shall be within it
48.11: And it will be for the Priests who are sanctified from the sons of Tsadoq, for they kept my observances and they have not strayed as the children of Israel have strayed, and like the Levites have strayed
48.12: And they shall have a division from the division of the land, The Holy of Holies, in the border of the Levites
48.13: And the Levites next to the border of the Priests: the length, twenty and five thousand, and the width, ten thousand, and all their space, twenty and five thousand, and the width, ten thousand
48.14: They shall not sell any of it and they shall not exchange it, and they shall not do away with the first fruit of the land, because its holiness is of LORD JEHOVAH
48.15: And five thousand that is left next to the entrance, twenty and five thousand. The place is for the city, for the inhabitants and for the suburbs, that the city will be within it
48.16: And these are the measurements toward the north wind: four thousand and five hundred; and to the east, four thousand and five hundred, and to the south, four thousand and five hundred, and to the west, four thousand and five hundred
48.17: And the suburbs of the city will be to the north, two hundred and fifty, and to the south, two hundred and fifty, and to the east, two hundred and fifty, and to the west, two hundred and fifty
48.18: And what is left next to the dedication of Holiness: ten thousand to the east, and ten thousand to the west; it shall be next to the dedication of Holiness, and its harvest food shall be for those who work for the city
48.19: And he who works for the city shall be enlisted from all the tribes of Israel
48.20: The whole division is twenty and five thousand by twenty and five thousand, and set the division of Holiness apart from the inheritance of the city
48.21: And what is left shall be for the Ruler of the city, and from the dedication of Holiness and from the inheritance of the city to the border of the east, twenty and five thousand, and to the West, twenty and five thousand, to the border of the west, next to the division of the Ruler, and the dedication of Holiness and the Sanctuary of the house shall be within it
48.22: And some of the inheritance of the Levites, and some of the inheritance of the city, the portion of the Ruler, will be in the city between the border of Yehuda and the border of Benyamin
48.23: But the rest of the tribes from the East wind and unto the west wind of Benyamin
48.24: And opposite the border of Benyamin from the east wind and unto the west wind of Shemon
48.25: And opposite the border of Shemon from the east wind and unto the west wind of Issakar
48.26: And opposite the border of Issakar from the east wind and unto the west wind of Zebulon
48.27: And opposite the border of Zebulon from the east wind and unto the west wind of Gad
48.28: And opposite the border of Gad, from the south wind, shall be the border of Tamar unto the waters of Maribath Qadish, his inheritance unto the Great Sea
48.29: This is the land that you shall divide an inheritance to the tribes of Israel, and these are their divisions, says THE LORD OF LORDS
48.30: And these are the exits of the city from the north wind: the measurement, four thousand and five hundred
48.31: And the gates of the city by the names of the tribes of the children of Israel: on the north, three gates: one of Rubil and one of Yehuda and one of Levi
48.32: And on the east, the measure is four thousand and five hundred, and three gates: one of Yoseph and one of Benyamin and one of Dan
48.33: And on the south, the measure is four thousand and five hundred, and three gates: one of Shemon and one of Issakar and one of Zebulon
48.34: And on the west, the measure was four thousand and five hundred, and three gates: one of Gad and one of Ashayr and one of Naphtali
48.35: And around it eighteen thousand, and the name of the city is from the days LORD JEHOVAH named it
